mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity pricing by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, so lower FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation accelerates while the policy rate is still near zero, real-rate expectations rise and the market reprices tighter future policy, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% m/m for 2 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS < 0.50%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered events do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if more than 60% of triggered cases are positive despite the inflation-and-low-rate condition.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and demand, so a month-over-month increase in UNRATE predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points versus 3 months ago
failure_condition: Reject if the signal fails to produce negative median 20-day returns in triggered periods, or if its 3-year rolling hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized improvement in payrolls and retail sales indicates stronger final demand and earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal does not outperform either proxy alone, or if positive 20-day SPX returns are absent when both series trend upward for 2 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production is expanding and housing starts are improving, firms face stronger current output and investment conditions, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both rise month over month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint expansion condition does not correspond to positive median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal is neutral once either series is removed.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly pickup in real GDP growth proxies stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which should lift equity prices over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP upswings fail to associate with positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal reverses sign during two consecutive expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger payroll gain combined with a falling unemployment rate signals durable labor-market improvement, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next week through faster earnings expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls in the same monthly release
failure_condition: Reject if the same-release improvement does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal disappears after controlling for broad market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in retail sales growth while housing starts remain weak signals demand deceleration that should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines for 2 consecutive months and HOUST is below its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if more than half of triggered cases are positive despite synchronized demand weakness.
